---
title: "Simulated data example"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Simulated data example}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

## Simulate data with the package utility

```{r}
library(stressPLS)

sim <- simulate_formative_hoc_data(n = 50, indicators_per_construct = 3, seed = 42)
dat <- sim$data
model <- sim$model
```

## Generate stress scenarios

```{r}
indicator_grid <- perturb_indicators(model, method = "leave_one_out",
                                     constructs = c("Image", "Quality"))
indicator_grid
```

## Fit with an example-only backend

This toy backend is deterministic and exists only to demonstrate the interface.
It is not a PLS-SEM estimator.

```{r}
toy_backend <- function(model, data, scenario = NULL, ...) {
  brand_score <- rowMeans(data[c("img1", "img2", "img3", "qual1", "qual2", "qual3")])
  sat_score <- rowMeans(data[c("sat1", "sat2", "sat3")])
  estimate <- unname(coef(lm(sat_score ~ brand_score))[2])
  list(paths = data.frame(from = "BrandEquity", to = "Satisfaction",
                          estimate = estimate))
}

fit_grid <- fit_perturbation_grid(model, dat, indicator_grid, toy_backend)
calc_path_stability(fit_grid)
```
